macro: The Fed has held its benchmark rate at 3.50%-3.75% for four straight meetings, but CME FedWatch pricing on rate-hike odds for the July 29 decision jumped to 46.5% this week, up sharply from a roughly 70% probability of a hold just days earlier, as renewed US-Iran fighting over the Strait of Hormuz pushed oil prices higher and revived inflation risk. The Bureau of Labor Statistics released its June CPI report this morning; consensus had looked for the annual rate to ease toward 3.8% from May's 4.2%, though economists cautioned any improvement reflected a one-month reversal in energy prices that has already begun to unwind with this week's Hormuz escalation. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh delivers his first semiannual Monetary Policy testimony to the House Financial Services Committee today, followed by the Senate Banking Committee tomorrow. Warsh's remarks on the rate path, arriving alongside today's inflation print and the oil shock, are the single most important event to watch this week.
btc_status: Bitcoin traded near $62,550 as of 6 a.m. ET today, sitting just above its 200-week moving average (last confirmed near $61,300 in early June), with the Crypto Fear & Greed Index reading approximately 28 (Fear) as of Jul 13. The key level to watch is $65,200 resistance (the 200-day moving average) against $58,115 support (this cycle's monthly low).

HUM — Stock has more than doubled since the March 30 war-driven market bottom; RSI above 70 and among the most overbought names in the S&P 500
energy: WTI traded near $79.56 a barrel, up about 2% on the session, while Brent jumped to $86.99, up $8.68 from the prior session, after renewed US-Iran strikes shut transit through the Strait of Hormuz. The EIA's most recent weekly report (week ended Jul 3, released Jul 8) showed US crude inventories rising 2.998 million barrels, the first build after ten straight weekly draws, leaving stocks about 6% below the five-year average. The Hormuz closure remains the dominant supply risk, with maritime traffic through the strait down sharply from typical daily crossings.
vix_and_dollar: The VIX's last confirmed close was 15.03 (Jul 10), calm by historical standards, though it spiked intraday to roughly 17.40 on Monday as Hormuz tensions flared — a move not yet reflected in an official close. The Dollar Index (DXY) eased about 0.18% to near 101.10. The combination of an unconfirmed volatility pickup and a softer dollar suggests investors are pricing geopolitical risk unevenly across asset classes rather than de-risking broadly.
mortgage_rate: The 30-year fixed mortgage rate averaged 6.49% for the week of Jul 9, 2026, per Freddie Mac's PMMS survey, up slightly from 6.43% the week prior. Rates remain range-bound in the mid-6% area, keeping affordability pressure on buyers even as they sit below the year-ago level near 6.72%.
personal_finance_deep_dive: The gap between having money and having accessible money keeps widening: a U.S. News survey of 1,216 adults fielded in mid-January 2026 found 43% could not cover a $1,000 emergency expense from savings, even as separate 2026 polling shows roughly a quarter of Americans hold no emergency fund at all. Renewed inflation pressure, echoed in this week's oil-driven CPI dynamics, is compounding the problem — over half of respondents in recent surveys say rising prices are actively eroding what cushion they do have. Lower- and middle-income households are disproportionately exposed, since they carry less of a buffer between routine expenses and unplanned ones. The pattern points to a structural liquidity problem sitting underneath otherwise-resilient aggregate income figures.
wealth_building_deep_dive: Vanguard's S&P 500 ETF (VOO) became the first exchange-traded fund in history to cross $1 trillion in net assets on Jun 2, 2026, a milestone built almost entirely on ordinary investors making the same small, repeated decision: routing a slice of each paycheck into a diversified index fund on a fixed schedule, regardless of headlines. That consistency is the mechanism, not the ticker — the same dollar-cost-averaging discipline works through a 401(k), an IRA, or a taxable brokerage account. A reader can apply it this week by automating a fixed transfer into a low-cost S&P 500 fund on payday, rather than waiting for a better entry point; the data suggests timing matters far less than showing up on schedule.
notable: Five major banks — JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Bank of America, Wells Fargo and Citigroup — report Q2 earnings today, with options markets pricing implied moves of 4.4%-6.0%.
SK Hynix's record $26.5B Nasdaq listing continues to reverberate; the Procure Space ETF is on track for its fifth negative week in six as investors digest a separate incoming SpaceX IPO.
Humana and other health insurers are flagged as overbought, with sector RSI readings at levels not seen since July 2024.
